message:
fix(synology-chat): harden webhook payload parsing with typed field extractors

Replace generic required_payload_scalar/optional_payload_scalar with
type-specific extractors that enforce correct value handling:

- required_payload_string: rejects non-string values (catches boolean
  tokens that previously passed as "true")
- required_payload_string_or_integer: accepts string or integer IDs
  (channel_id, user_id, post_id, thread_id can be numeric in some
  Synology Chat versions)
- optional_payload_string: strict string-only extraction
- required_payload_i64: add non-negative validation for timestamps

The token field now requires an actual string match — previously a
boolean true would silently coerce to "true" and potentially match
a configured token string, which is a type confusion vulnerability.

Add integration tests:
- ingest_outgoing_webhook_rejects_non_string_token_values
- ingest_outgoing_webhook_rejects_negative_timestamps

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
